original_image_tags: Scanned back of a thin, yellowish paper document. Multiple ink stamps and faint bleed-through text from the front side are visible. Three hole-punch circles are at the top edge. The paper appears to be a routing slip or back of a memo.
A red ink stamp that is partially illegible, possibly reading "144" or similar, stamped at an angle.

elements.description: Faintly visible reverse text and multiple ink stamps on aged paper, including 'REC'D MAIL ROOM F.B.I. Jul 21 5 27 PM '66', 'RECEIVED DIRECTOR F.B.I. Jul 20 3 27 PM '66', and 'REC'D DETACH FBI Jul 20 10 09 AM '66'. Two black circular hole-punches at the top.

judge.reason: Description A describes a single, partially illegible red stamp possibly reading '144', while Description B quotes three distinct, legible stamps with specific text and dates, representing a major contradiction on the verbatim content of the stamps.
